title: Cell Culture and UV Cross-Linking
author: Clémentine elan-Forino

steps_raw.instruction: Streak out HTP-tagged and negative control strains from glycerol stocks onto YPD plates (or SD medium—leucine when required for plasmid maintenance) and incubate at 30 °C for 48:00:00 – 76:00:00 .

Inoculate single colonies into 5 mL, SD medium (YNB−amino acids, CSM−TRP, and 2% glucose) and incubate at 30 °C with shaking at 200 rpm, °C, for 06:00:00 , then use to inoculate 100 mL, overnight cultures .
Inoculate 2.75 L, prewarmed minimal media with the quantity of overnight culture required to obtain an OD 600 around 0.05, and grow until the OD 600 measurement reaches 0.5.
Clean the UV cross-linker with deionized water before use, and prewarm the lamp.
Irradiate cultures in the UV cross-linker at Room temperature for 00:01:40 (254 nm, equivalent to an average dose of ∼1.6 mJ cm −2 ).
Pour the cultures into prechilled 1L centrifuge bottles On ice . Harvest by centrifugation; 2700 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes .

Resuspend pellet in 30 mL, ice-cold phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) and transfer to a 50 ml Falcon tube. Centrifuge for 3000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . We usually keep the three pellets in individual tubes and use one per CRAC experiment.
Discard supernatant and store pellets at -80 °C until required.
